purpose: Fixes a potential use-after-free vulnerability by checking if HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set before queuing commands. If set, it returns an error instead of proceeding with command queuing.
Removes an unnecessary include of <asm/termios.h> from drivers/tty/n_hdlc.c, as <linux/tty.h> already includes <linux/termios.h>. The change simplifies the include structure and avoids redundancy.

This commit updates documentation for the perf c2c tool to reflect the addition of an 'N/A' metric for store operations. It clarifies that the 'N/A' category represents store accesses where the memory level is not available, enhancing the accuracy of the report table.

Fixes race conditions and synchronization issues in huge page memory management during VMA splitting and range unmapping. Addresses problems with vma lock handling, particularly when VMAs are duplicated during splitting, and ensures proper locking during pmd sharing.

This commit adds documentation for two power supply regulators (AVDD and MICVDD) used by the rt5682s audio codec. It updates the device tree binding file to require these supplies and provides examples of their usage.

Fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx SCSI driver by adding a missing iounmap() call in the remove function. The commit addresses an error handling path where a resource allocated during probe was not freed during removal, specifically when host->base > 0x01000000.
Removes an unused work field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the Mellanox kTLS implementation. The change is minimal and purely structural, eliminating dead code without altering functionality.

The commit changes the data type of key_idx from u8 (unsigned 8-bit) to s8 (signed 8-bit) in the cfg80211_auth_request struct. This allows key_idx to hold the value -1, which is used when the key is NULL, avoiding warnings from static checkers like smatch.

The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfig files. It removes commented-out CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS lines, likely to align with savedefconfig output format. No functional changes expected.

Introduces a new helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check CAN-FD mode and replaces open-coded checks. The change centralizes logic for determining FD mode, improving code clarity and maintainability.
Commit modifies RTC selftests to skip execution when RTC device is not present, instead of failing. Adds conditional skip logic for ENOENT error case across multiple test functions.

Removes a redundant variable assignment in ALSA echoaudio MIDI code that was causing a clang scan build warning. The variable 'bytes' is assigned but never read before being reassigned in a subsequent if block.

This commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks by removing the TASKS_RCU dependency in Kconfig and conditionally compiling RCU Tasks support in rcuscale.c. It allows rcuscale to be built without RCU Tasks, reducing kernel size and complexity.

This commit removes a duplicated word 'be split' from a comment in the KVM x86 mmu code, specifically in spte.h. The change is purely textual and affects only a documentation comment, not the actual functionality.

Reverts changes to populate DCN315 clock table based on DCFCLK. The commit addresses a no-read situation caused by PMFW filling the table when DCFCLK states are disabled, leading to potential underflow. It reverts to hardcoded values to prevent issues until a better solution is implemented.

Adds support for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU by documenting its compatible string in the device tree bindings. The change updates the YAML schema to include the new compatible string and adjusts validation rules.

Rename struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for consistency with naming conventions in DW PCIe driver. The change affects function signatures and variable declarations across multiple files, maintaining identical functionality while improving code consistency.
Fixes a clang compiler warning by correcting the format specifier for a ntohs() return value. The change updates %hx to %x to match the promoted int type of the argument.
The commit fixes a branch range check in MIPS jump_label code by casting the upper bound to long for signed comparison, preventing a warning triggered by negative offsets.
The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU() and calls the function directly. It's part of a larger effort to eliminate unwanted HAL layer code.

The commit fixes a sparse warning by declaring a non-global symbol as static in the ARM64 kernel code. It addresses a specific issue where a symbol was not properly declared as static, causing a compiler warning.

This commit corrects incorrect Devicetree interrupt flag specifications for MDSS display subsystem nodes. It removes bogus interrupt flags cells from consumer nodes, as the MDSS interrupt provider is a single cell, making the flags specification incorrect.

The commit refactors array allocation logic in V4L2 controls, renaming dynamic array handling to generic array handling and preparing for driver-controlled array sizing. It modifies internal data structures and allocation functions to support more flexible array management.

Adds support for new PMICs and SoCs to the Qualcomm socinfo driver. The commit maps new device IDs to their respective model names and SoC names, enabling proper identification of hardware platforms and power management chips.
The commit updates the RTL8xxxu WiFi driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (A-P) instead of showing 'unknown' for cuts beyond E. It changes from a switch-case to a direct character calculation.

The commit removes the custom software enable count ('is_enabled') from PRCMU clocks, relying instead on the core's existing software enable count. It eliminates redundant state tracking and related enable/disable functions.

This commit reorganizes #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage in RISC-V memory management initialization code. It moves macro definitions closer to their usage and groups related conditional code, improving code structure and readability without changing functional behavior.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree for the sm6350 Lena board, preparing for future I2C functionality by activating DMA controllers.

This commit fixes comments and documentation in the rzg2l thermal driver. It corrects a misleading comment, fixes a typo ('caliberation' to 'calibration'), and adjusts comment formatting to match coding style. No functional code changes are present.

Fixes an infinite loop in the igc driver's release_swfw_sync function when hardware semaphore acquisition fails. The change replaces a busy-wait loop with a check that logs an error and returns if the semaphore cannot be acquired, preventing system hang during surprise removal.

The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, replacing 'master_mode' with 'provider_mode' and updating related clocking definitions to align with current kernel standards.

Removes a pointless code hack for IS_SWAPFILE that was always defined. The change eliminates a conditional compilation block that defined IS_SWAPFILE to 0 when not already defined, which is no longer necessary.

Implement GTP echo response functionality to handle GTP echo requests when no userspace daemon is present. Adds support for GTPv0 and GTPv1 echo responses with proper recovery information elements and restart counter handling.

The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, and adds a new cpumask_first_zero function. The changes improve performance by using more efficient mask operations, particularly when starting from index -1.

Removes a call to the 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR that fails in firmware, eliminating error messages during STA roaming. The change removes code that attempted to fetch TIM information from firmware after roaming, which is no longer supported.

Adds support for a new board configuration 'adl_rt5682' that connects alc5682 codec to SSP0 without speaker amplifier, following Intel BT offload design. Introduces new platform device ID and ACPI match entry.

This commit ensures zerocopy sendmsg operations fail gracefully when the underlying socket does not support zerocopy. It adds a check for SOCK_SUPPORT_ZC flag and returns -EOPNOTSUPP if unsupported, aligning behavior with previous zerocopy send handling.

This commit modifies the LaTeX preamble used for generating kernel documentation. It adds logic to test for the existence of xeCJK.sty before loading it, preventing xelatex errors when the package is missing. The change improves robustness for systems with fine-grained TeXLive packaging.

This commit modifies the checkpatch.pl script to allow 'case' macros without reporting them as errors. It adjusts the validation logic to exclude case statements from the parentheses requirement check.

Converts simpledrm driver from simple-KMS helpers to atomic helpers for better architecture and code sharing. Replaces drm_simple_display_pipe with explicit drm_plane, drm_crtc, and drm_encoder structures and helper functions. No functional changes.
Adds JPEG decode worker interface to enable parallel execution of three hardware units on MT8195. Implements hardware resource management, workqueue handling, and multi-core decoding support.

Removes VPRINTK() debug calls and ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function from ATA subsystem. The changes eliminate verbose debug output that was redundant with tracepoints, improving code clarity and reducing overhead.

This commit adds support for zstate optimization during extended vblank periods in AMD FreeSync implementation. It enables entering zstate (power management states) when extra vblank time is available during FreeSync transitions, improving power efficiency. The change involves new logic to calculate available time and conditionally enable zstate entry based on thresholds.
Fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() function callback parameter, aligning the function pointer prototype with actual implementation to resolve Clang's CFI failure.

Corrects a typo in the Devicetree binding property name from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' for validation. This ensures proper schema validation and correct interpretation of reset properties.
The commit silences an array-bounds warning in the ath9k wireless driver when compiled with GCC 12. It adds a temporary compiler flag to suppress the -Warray-bounds warning for non-W=1+ builds.

The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss PRNG driver. This simplifies the code by combining the resume and get operations into a single call, reducing redundancy and improving maintainability.

This commit refactors io_uring file handling by splitting fixed file installation and removal logic into the filetable module. It changes function signatures to use io_ring_ctx instead of io_kiocb, improving code organization and reducing coupling.

The commit splits a single device tree binding for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU into separate G1 and G2 decoder nodes, reflecting that they are independent hardware blocks sharing only a power domain. It updates the DT schema and examples to support separate nodes with proper power-domain references.

The commit removes a redundant macro IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON and inlines its contents into IWL_DEVICE_BZ. This is a minor refactoring that simplifies the code structure by eliminating one level of indirection.
